text:
September 3rd: Part 10 (93⑩, Kugatsu Mikka (Jū)) is the 94 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

As Gon and Killua kick through the side walls, Nobunaga is left stunned by their plan and quickly wonders which one he should follow. He thinks about the building's layout and says that they are on the fifth floor, with Killua closest to the stairwell. As Nobunaga opens the door and decides to go after Gon, he hears the lock to the room next door click and instead heads through the hole that Gon used to escape. However, after hearing Gon and Killua smashing through more walls, he decides to follow Gon since Killua is making his way down the stairs.

Nobunaga quickens his pace when he hears Gon smash through another wall, but is left surprised when he finds the final room empty and its door blocked. As he wonders if Gon is hiding, Gon suddenly shouts Killua's name from the hallway, and Nobunaga realizes that he used the last hole as a decoy, doubling back and then hiding before he escaped. Gon yells to Killua that they will fight Nobunaga together, but Nobunaga suddenly kicks down the door and clears the debris. He finds the hallway empty and doesn't sense their auras, offended that they would try to ambush him from the shadows. He then uses a technique called En, which is explained as an advanced application of Ten and Ren. It's added that while aura usually covers a Nen user's body with a layer no more than a few centimeters thick, En increases that layer to at least a few meters.

Nobunaga says that he can sense the movement of everything within his 4-meter striking range, but as he waits for the boys to attack him, the two are seen already far away from the building. Killua calls Gon an idiot, but Gon replies that he really wanted to beat him. Killua assures him that Nobunaga would have easily killed them before they could even touch him since they only know the Nen basics. Gon gives a big smile and says that he's glad Killua is back to being himself, adding that he's relying on him since he's the reckless one.

Gon then wonders what they should do next, declaring that he wants to fight them, and Killua remembers that three of the Phantom Troupe members are worth 6 billion in bounty money. However, he tells Gon that they first need to improve their Nen and that they should ask Kurapika. Gon wonders why, and Killua reveals that he's the chain user mentioned by the Troupe, surprised that Gon hadn't figured it out. He points out that Kurapika learned Nen at the same time as they did and stresses to Gon that they need unique abilities, adding that they need to be able to hold their own against the Troupe. As the two board a train to head back to the city, a hungry Milluki is also seen at a train station.

Four hours before Gon and Killua escaped from Nobunaga, Neon is seen shopping at Lingon Airport as Melody and Basho struggle to carry all the items she bought. As the two bond over how draining it all is, Neon watches a group of girls going to the bathroom and says that she has to go, asking Melody and Basho to watch her things. Basho worries about Kurapika, and Melody says that her flute should make him feel less tired. He then asks about the hit job on the Troupe, and Melody says that Kurapika only agreed to join because he's willing to sacrifice everything for his goal.

As the group of girls leaves the bathroom, Basho tells Melody that he just wants to ride his bike around the world, wondering why Neon is taking so long. Eliza offers to go check and suddenly comes running back out, telling them that no one is in there. Neon thanks the group of girls and takes off a black wig she had used as a disguise, telling herself that she always knows her father is lying when he uses the word "promise". As she decides to go to the auction by herself since he doesn't want to take her, a person holding a picture of Neon is seen watching her from behind.
